title: 3,000+ Tokens Per Second? Let's Stress Test Xiaomi's New Model: MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed
summary: Xiaomi and TileRT released MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed, a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model achieving over 1,000 tokens per second on standard hardware. The model uses selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a TileRT persistent GPU kernel runtime to optimize inference speed.
writeup: Better Stack stress tests MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed, a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model from Xiaomi and TileRT that targets over 1,000 tokens per second on standard hardware. The video covers the three optimizations behind that throughput: selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and TileRT, a persistent GPU kernel runtime. It matters because it shows how MoE sparsity combined with inference-level techniques can push token generation speed on commodity GPUs rather than relying on larger or specialized hardware.
